untranslatable_reason: BARR Bottom is a visual candlestick pattern (inverted frying pan/spoon formation) that requires pattern recognition algorithms to identify. The specific shape, structure, and momentum characteristics of the pattern cannot be expressed using only the allowed price-based indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, ATR, etc.) without a dedicated pattern detection function. Entry condition requires explicit pattern identification which is not available in the indicator set.
translation_notes: The signal describes a momentum-based reversal pattern at market bottoms with a 62-98% success rate in bull markets and <2% failure rate. A placeholder breakout condition (close above previous high) was provided, but this does not capture the visual/structural essence of the BARR Bottom formation. Exit conditions were not specified (AUTHOR_SILENT), so a mean reversion exit below SMA_20 was inferred. Throwback/pullback negatively impacts performance post-breakout but cannot be explicitly modeled without pattern state tracking.
